evidence_text: The study analyzes a curated AIDev subset of 33,596 agent-authored pull requests from popular GitHub repositories.
source_excerpt: "We conduct a large-scale empirical analysis of agent-authored PRs using the AIDev dataset, comprising of over 33,000 curated PRs from popular GitHub repositories."
note: The paper elsewhere specifies the curated subset size as 33,596 PRs.
caveat: The paper elsewhere specifies the curated subset size as 33,596 PRs.

evidence_text: Security-related PR candidates were first identified by keyword filtering on PR titles and descriptions, yielding 2,598 candidate PRs.
source_excerpt: "After this filtering step, we initially identify 2,598 candidate security-related agentic authored PRs in the curated AIDev subset."
note: The filter used case-insensitive regular expressions with word-boundary constraints.
caveat: The filter used case-insensitive regular expressions with word-boundary constraints.

evidence_text: Manual vetting reduced 2,598 keyword-matched candidates to 1,293 security-related PRs.
source_excerpt: "Based on this manual validation, we identified 1,293 security-related PRs."
note: The source says PRs without keyword matches were not manually reviewed, so some security-relevant PRs may have been missed.
caveat: The source says PRs without keyword matches were not manually reviewed, so some security-relevant PRs may have been missed.

evidence_text: The study found 1,293 security-related PRs, equal to 3.85% of 33,596 agent-authored PRs in the curated dataset.
source_excerpt: "From the curated AIDev dataset, we identified 1,293 security-related PRs. These 1,293 PRs represent 3.85% of all 33,596 Agentic-PRs in the curated dataset."

evidence_text: Claude Code had the highest reported proportion of security-relevant PRs at 14.6%.
citation: Siddiq et al., 2026, highlights and Section 4.1
source_excerpt: "We quantify the prevalence of security-relevant Agentic-PRs, identifying 1,293 confirmed cases (3.85% of agent activity), with a variation across agents and Claude Code exhibiting the highest proportion (14.6%)."
note: Section 4.1 also lists counts and percentages for other agents, but one sentence reports counts that appear inconsistent with the total curated subset and should be read cautiously.
caveat: Section 4.1 also lists counts and percentages for other agents, but one sentence reports counts that appear inconsistent with the total curated subset and should be read cautiously.

evidence_text: The source says agents most often perform supportive security hardening work such as testing, documentation, configuration, and improved error handling rather than only narrow vulnerability fixes.
citation: Siddiq et al., 2026, Abstract and RQ3
source_excerpt: "Rather than focusing solely on narrow vulnerability fixes, agents most frequently perform supportive security hardening activities, including testing, documentation, configuration, and improved error handling."

evidence_text: Among coded security actions, Code Refactoring appeared 957 times, Testing 755 times, and Documentation 692 times.
source_excerpt: "Table 4: Security actions and intents identified via open coding ... Actions ... Code Refactoring 957 Testing 755 Documentation 692"

evidence_text: Among coded security intents, Functionality Improvement appeared 890 times and Vulnerability Mitigation 741 times.
OpenAI Codex had the highest reported merge rate for security-related PRs at 86.59%, while Copilot had the lowest at 49.60%.
source_excerpt: "As observed in this figure, we found that OpenAI Codex achieves the highest merge rate (86.59%) ... while Copilot (49.60%) demonstrates the lowest merge rate."

evidence_text: TypeScript had the largest number of security PRs in the top-language table with 447 PRs and a 56.51% merge rate.
source_excerpt: "While TypeScript accounts for the largest numbers of security PRs (447 PRs), it exhibits a comparatively lower merge rate (56.51%)"

evidence_text: Rust security PRs had the lowest merge rate in the top-language table at 51.16%.
source_excerpt: "Notably, Rust security PRs have the lowest merge rate (51.16%)"

evidence_text: Security Feature PRs were the largest security-change category with 554 PRs and a 64.61% merge rate.
note: In Table 3, the first column corresponds to Security Feature.
caveat: In Table 3, the first column corresponds to Security Feature.

evidence_text: Security-related PRs had a lower merge rate than non-security PRs: 61.50% versus 77.33%.
source_excerpt: "security-relevant Agentic-PRs have consistently lower merge rates than non-security PRs i.e. ,  security PRs are merged at only 61.50% , compared to 77.33% for non-security PRs"

evidence_text: Security-related PRs had a median review latency of 3.92 hours versus 0.11 hours for non-security PRs, and the difference was reported as statistically significant.
source_excerpt: "Security PRs have a median review latency of 3.92 hours, compared to only 0.11 hours for non-security PRs ... This difference is statistically significant ( p < 0.001 )"
note: The paper treats review latency as a conservative proxy for reviewer scrutiny rather than a direct measure of reviewer intent.
caveat: The paper treats review latency as a conservative proxy for reviewer scrutiny rather than a direct measure of reviewer intent.

evidence_text: Early rejection of security-related PRs was more strongly associated with complexity and verbosity signals than with explicit security keywords, and text-based models outperformed structured-feature models for rejection prediction.
citation: Siddiq et al., 2026, Table 8 and Table 9
source_excerpt: "We conduct a large-scale empirical analysis of agent-authored PRs using the AIDev dataset, comprising of over 33,000 curated PRs from popular GitHub repositories."
note: The paper elsewhere specifies the curated subset size as 33,596 PRs.
caveat: The paper elsewhere specifies the curated subset size as 33,596 PRs.
